introSection:
Little Dipper is a junior steel roller coaster located at Conneaut Lake Park in Conneaut Lake, Pennsylvania, United States.

The coaster was built by the Allan Herschell Company and began operating in 1950. It has one three-car train. Riders are seated two across with two rows per car, giving the coaster a capacity of 12 riders. The coaster closed alongside the park at the end of the 2019 season, and has not operated since. It is still standing as of 2025, and its train is still parked in the station.
fullText:
Little Dipper is a junior steel roller coaster located at Conneaut Lake Park in Conneaut Lake, Pennsylvania, United States.

The coaster was built by the Allan Herschell Company and began operating in 1950. It has one three-car train. Riders are seated two across with two rows per car, giving the coaster a capacity of 12 riders. The coaster closed alongside the park at the end of the 2019 season, and has not operated since. It is still standing as of 2025, and its train is still parked in the station.
